video_title: How JTX Plans to Move Price Discovery Onchain
gist: Jito founder Lucas Bruder explains JTX, a CEX-grade 'prosumer' trading terminal Jito is launching on Solana that leans on Jito's BAM block-building infrastructure and Solana's new generation of proprietary market-maker AMMs ('prop AMMs') to deliver sub-basis-point spreads and route 80% of trading fees into JTO buybacks. He argues Solana's prop-AMM architecture is finally solving on-chain price discovery for major assets and tokenized equities, and that SOL is undervalued given accelerating protocol iteration and pending deflationary tokenomics changes.

insights.claim: Tokenized stocks on Solana have on some days generated higher trading volume than meme coins, marking a shift from Solana's reputation as primarily a meme-coin chain toward legitimate tokenized-equity issuance.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: A volume mix shifting from meme coins to tokenized equities is a concrete, trackable signal of Solana's transition toward more durable, institutional-adjacent asset flow.
insights.quote: There's a few days where tokenized stock volume is higher than meme coins, and it's cool to see that flip.

insights.claim: Backpack currently leads Solana's tokenized-equity venues by trading volume despite listing only around five assets versus competitors offering tens or hundreds, suggesting the market-share picture in tokenized stocks is still unsettled and likely to shift as rivals expand asset coverage.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Early leadership in tokenized-equity volume could flip quickly as competitors like HXRO and Ondo scale up listed-asset counts.

themes.theme: JTX: A Prosumer Trading Terminal for Solana

insights.claim: Jito is building JTX, a 'prosumer' trading terminal that deliberately hides token-level metadata (holder distribution, freeze status) that meme-coin traders obsess over, targeting traders of roughly $1M+ market-cap tokens, tokenized equities, and major assets rather than brand-new meme-coin launches.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: JTX is a deliberate segment bet away from degen meme trading toward institutional-adjacent flow, which could pull volume from centralized exchanges rather than from Solana's existing meme platforms.
insights.quote: We're not building for the token that launched 5 seconds ago where you need to know the distribution.

insights.claim: Average hold times on existing Solana meme-coin trading platforms are under a minute, and rarely exceed 10 minutes even at the upper end, which Jito cites as evidence that current Solana retail UX is built for ultra-short-term speculation rather than the investor-grade experience JTX targets.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Quantifies how speculative current Solana retail flow is today, giving investors a baseline to judge whether JTX actually shifts trading behavior toward longer holds.
insights.quote: the average hold time is like less than a minute. And like at the upper end of it, it's like less than 10 minutes.

insights.claim: JTX uses Privy for key management, letting users sign in via email or set up passkeys instead of managing seed phrases, aiming for onboarding friction closer to a centralized exchange than a typical Solana DEX.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Lower onboarding friction is a testable adoption lever; watch JTX signup conversion versus wallet-based Solana DEX competitors.
insights.quote: We're using privy for key pair management so you can sign in through email.

insights.claim: JTX will not launch with perpetuals but plans to integrate Phoenix Perps shortly after spot launch, followed by prediction markets, once the core spot trading experience is in a good state.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: A Phoenix Perps integration announcement would be the next expansion catalyst to watch for Jito's consumer product suite.
insights.quote: It won't be a launch, but shortly after launch, we are looking at Phoenix perps and we'll be integrating that.

insights.claim: JTX is positioned to eventually compete directly with centralized exchanges and traditional venues like Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange over a multi-year horizon, not just with other Solana apps such as Jupiter or pump.fun, even though the near-term go-to-market is focused narrowly on the existing Solana-native, crypto-Twitter audience.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Signals Jito's total addressable market ambition extends far beyond crypto-native trading, which is relevant context for long-term JTO valuation.
insights.quote: I would say the centralized exchanges are our main competition.

insights.claim: Jito is considering building an in-house custodial fiat on/off-ramp (debit card, credit card, bank account) for JTX to avoid the high fees third-party on-ramps charge non-custodial platforms to cover fraud risk, though this idea was raised by the host and Lucas characterized it as still exploratory.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: A custodial on-ramp would be a significant strategic pivot for a non-custodial-focused team; worth watching for a formal product announcement.
insights.quote: rolling it in-house could be interesting. There's definitely that would be something completely new for us.

themes.theme: Jito's Infrastructure Edge Feeds JTX Execution

insights.claim: Jito has been the number one validator client and block builder on Solana since 2022 and holds the deepest liquidity in its liquid staking token JitoSOL, which the company frames as the credibility base for verticalizing from infrastructure into the consumer-facing JTX trading app.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Infrastructure dominance being leveraged into a new consumer revenue line is a template other infra-first crypto companies may replicate; JTX's success is a proxy for whether that leverage works.
insights.quote: we're the number one validator client block builder on Solana since like 2022.

insights.claim: Inside Jito's BAM block-building system, a 'maker priority plugin' places proprietary market makers (prop AMMs) at the top of the block across Solana assets, letting them quote tighter spreads without fear of being sandwiched or picked off.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Explains the structural mechanism behind Solana's tightening on-chain spreads — a block-building-level moat that competing chains would need to replicate to match execution quality.
insights.quote: all the uh makers for these prop AMMs across all these different assets on Solana, they basically get put at the top of the block on Solana in inside the BAM blocks.

insights.claim: JTX includes a 'Good Trade' feature that, on every swap, simulates the same trade on top centralized exchanges in real time and shows the user how much cheaper their Solana execution was, turning Solana's execution-quality claim into a per-trade, user-visible metric rather than a Twitter talking point.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: If the savings shown are consistently positive, this becomes a powerful retention and marketing tool; worth tracking published Good Trade savings data after launch.
insights.quote: we will look at the output amount and we will run that same trade on the best centralized exchanges at the same time and show you how much money you saved on chain compared to Solana.

insights.claim: Jito selectively avoids routing JTX transactions to specific Solana validators known for exploitative behavior such as sandwiching, an opinionated infrastructure choice learned from operating BAM rather than treating all validators as interchangeable.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Reduces MEV-related slippage for JTX users, a differentiator versus routing-agnostic wallets or DEX front-ends.

themes.theme: Prop AMMs as the New On-Chain Price-Discovery Engine

insights.claim: A new generation of single-market-maker 'prop AMMs' (SolFi from the Ellipsis team, BisonFi run by Jump Crypto, Humidifi, Scorch, Tesera, succeeding the earlier Lifinity) put market-maker curve logic directly on-chain, and can reprice for roughly sub-100 compute units versus about 40,000 compute units needed to requote a position on an on-chain order book.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: This ~400x compute-cost advantage is the structural technical reason prop AMMs beat order books on Solana — a durable architectural edge, not a temporary liquidity phenomenon.
insights.quote: I think the cheapest prop AMM oracle update is like sub 100 cost units. Where if you want to make a trade on an order book, it's like 40,000.

insights.claim: Unlike traditional constant-product AMMs (e.g., Uniswap-style pools), which rely on external arbitrageurs to correct mispricing after the fact, prop AMMs have an active market maker adjusting the curve directly, making them structurally capable of genuine price discovery rather than merely tracking an external reference price.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Bearish read-through for vanilla constant-product AMMs as venues for new-asset price discovery going forward; capital and innovation should keep migrating to prop-AMM designs.
insights.quote: I think this is uh super bearish on just like traditional AMMs because those those can never do price discovery because they rely on people arbitraging the price to bring it in line.

insights.claim: Aggregated prop-AMM liquidity on the SOL/USDC pair (viewable on sandwich.me) currently shows sub-one-basis-point spreads for 100 SOL of depth, and Lucas expects similarly tight spreads to extend to Bitcoin, Zcash, Hyperliquid's token, and newly listed tokenized equities like SpaceX.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Sandwich.me provides a concrete, publicly checkable metric to monitor whether Solana's execution-quality claims hold up as more assets list.
insights.quote: you can see here that for 100 SOL depth on Solana right now, you can get less than a bip spread. That's just like insane.

insights.claim: The prop-AMM top-of-block 'maker priority' pattern pioneered on Solana via BAM is now spreading to Ethereum L2s like Base and to Ethereum itself, where block builders such as Titan give prop AMMs priority to produce spreads tighter than the tightest centralized exchange, and similar dynamics are emerging on Robinhood's chain.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: If the pattern spreads to EVM chains, Solana's current execution-quality edge could narrow over time — a competitive dynamic worth monitoring rather than assuming permanent.
insights.quote: that's been something really cool to see that like that's born on Solana and then now we're seeing it on L2s and um Ethereum

themes.theme: Toxic Prop AMM Behavior and Aggregator Safeguards

insights.claim: Some Solana prop AMMs previously engaged in 'bait transaction' tactics — posting an attractive quote and then pulling it before a retail trade could execute against it — a toxic pattern publicly called out by 0x/Matcha's Will Warren.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Illustrates a real adversarial-design risk within prop-AMM market structure that retail traders on Solana faced a few months prior to this conversation.
insights.quote: they would set the price with a bait transaction, but then they would yoink the bait transaction

insights.claim: The bait-transaction problem was mitigated primarily through public social shaming of offending market makers plus distribution-layer aggregators like Jupiter and DFlow throttling or disconnecting flow to prop AMMs exhibiting toxic behavior, and increasingly by aggregators moving their routing logic fully on-chain so quotes reference live chain state instead of a stale off-chain snapshot during the one-to-two-second settlement window.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Aggregator gatekeeping and on-chain routing function as an emerging self-policing mechanism in Solana market structure, reducing toxicity risk for retail flow without requiring new regulation.
insights.quote: we're seeing more uh, AMMs or more of these uh, aggregators put their actual routing logic onto Solana.

insights.claim: 80% of all JTX trading revenue will be used to buy JTO tokens on the open market and send them to the Jito DAO treasury rather than being collected as USDC or volatile fee tokens, with the remaining 20% reinvested into the business.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: JTX trading volume becomes a directly quantifiable driver of JTO buy pressure — a metric investors can track against JTO's price and FDV over time.
insights.quote: 80% of the revenue from JTX will go to the DAO.

insights.claim: JTO's fully diluted valuation was cited at roughly $600-700 million during the conversation, versus SOL's market cap discussed in the tens of billions, a gap the host frames as a potential asymmetric setup if JTX successfully routes meaningful trading-fee flow into JTO buy pressure.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: The JTO-to-SOL valuation gap is a concrete number for investors evaluating whether JTX's fee-to-buyback mechanism could re-rate JTO relative to SOL.
insights.quote: it's the FDV right now is probably like 600 or 700 million.

themes.theme: Solana Tokenomics Reform and the SOL Investment Thesis

insights.claim: Solana is weighing SIMD governance proposals that would roughly double the rate at which staking inflation decreases and introduce a resource-based transaction burn tied to network usage, rather than today's flat per-transaction burn, changes Lucas expects to reduce sell pressure and make SOL more deflationary as prop-AMM-driven transaction volume grows.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: A concrete governance catalyst to track — passage of these SIMDs would directly tighten SOL's token supply dynamics and reduce emissions-driven sell pressure.
insights.quote: there's one that will double disinflation.

insights.claim: Solana's current transaction/priority-fee burn is a flat fee driven purely by transaction count rather than computational intensity, meaning heavy and light transactions burn the same amount today — a design gap the pending resource-based-fee proposal aims to fix.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Quantifies a current inefficiency in SOL's fee/burn design; resolving it would let compute-heavy prop-AMM activity contribute proportionally more to burn.
insights.quote: Yes straight transaction volume.

insights.claim: Jito's CEO argues Solana is uniquely undervalued relative to the rest of crypto because it is iterating faster than any other ecosystem, citing that core client teams are set to roughly double average block size from 60 million to 100 million within about a month, alongside compounding improvements in tokenomics, prop-AMM execution, and tokenized-equity issuance.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: The block-size doubling within roughly a month is a concrete near-term technical catalyst to watch as a leading indicator for the broader Solana bull thesis.
insights.quote: we're going to go from 60 million to 100 million block size within the next month or so.

insights.claim: Roughly around the time of the Jito airdrop, Solana's transaction pipeline was unreliable enough that most users had to land transactions through Jito's infrastructure to succeed, illustrating how much core-protocol reliability has improved since then via client teams Anza and Firedancer alongside Jito.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Provides historical context for how recent and rapid Solana's reliability turnaround has been, useful for judging how durable the current execution-quality improvements are.
insights.quote: If you used the chain back then, it was just like a nightmare to use. Like transactions wouldn't land.

insights.claim: JTX access is being gated by referral count during rollout starting the week of July 14-15, 2026 — users in roughly the top 10% by referrals get earlier access — with the initial release explicitly described as an MVP ahead of planned data features, equities, perps, and prediction-market expansions.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: The referral-driven waitlist is itself a growth-loop distribution mechanic worth watching for early adoption and viral-growth metrics.
insights.quote: we're going by the number of referrals that you have. So if you're in the top like 10% or something, you'll get access earlier.

insights.claim: Lucas Bruder expects that within roughly 6 to 9 months, any stock from any country in the world will be tradable on-chain, contingent on regulatory clarity and a hoped-for SEC exemption.
insights.speaker: Lucas Bruder, Jito founder/CEO
insights.so_what: Sets a specific, checkable forward timeline for the global tokenized-equities buildout that investors can monitor for progress or slippage.
insights.quote: I think within the next probably 6 to 9 months, you will be able to trade any stock in any country in the world on chain.
